data.title: Bitcoin surpasses Saudi Aramco to become the eighth largest asset in the world
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily News: According to CompaniesMarketcap data, as the price of Bitcoin rebounded to the $85,000 range, its market value has now risen to $1.677 trillion, with a daily increase of 4.51%, surpassing Saudi Aramco (US$1.675 trillion) to rank eighth in the global asset market value.

data.title: Crypto.com Enters UAE Derivatives Market After Securing VARA Approval
data.description:
Crypto.com has secured a limited license from Dubai’s Virtual Assets Regulatory Authority (VARA) to offer derivatives trading in the UAE.

The post Crypto.com Enters UAE Derivatives Market After Securing VARA Approval first appeared on Ecoinimist.

source: https://ecoinimist.com/2025/03/14/crypto-com-gains-uae-license/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=crypto-com-gains-uae-license

data.title: Analyst: Whales bought more than 20,000 BTC in the past 48 hours
data.description:
Odaily Planet Daily News According to data disclosed by crypto analyst Ali Martinez, whales bought more than 20,000 BTC in the past 48 hours.
The market shows that BTC broke through 85,000 USDT in a short period of time today and is now reported at 84,942.1 USDT, with a 24-hour increase of 4.7%.

data.title: Bitcoin Surges as Whales Accumulate Over 20,000 BTC
data.description: According to Odaily, data revealed by crypto analyst Ali Martinez indicates that whales have purchased more than 20,000 BTC in the past 48 hours. The market shows that Bitcoin briefly surpassed 85,000 USDT today and is currently priced at 84,942.1 USDT, marking a 24-hour increase of 4.7%.

data.title: Hong Kong's Blockchain Sector Sees 250% Growth Since 2022
data.description:
According to PANews, a recent report by Hong Kong's InvestHK and its partners reveals a significant 250% growth in Hong Kong's blockchain applications and software industry since 2022. This surge underscores Hong Kong's emerging role as a leading center for fintech and digital asset development in Asia.

The report analyzed 1,100 fintech companies operating in Hong Kong as of July 2024, highlighting substantial growth across various fintech sub-sectors. Currently, Hong Kong hosts 175 blockchain companies, with the applications/software sector experiencing remarkable expansion over the past two years. Additionally, the digital assets and cryptocurrency segment has increased by nearly 30%.

Hong Kong now boasts the highest number of fintech companies in areas such as wealth technology, blockchain, digital assets and cryptocurrencies, payments and remittances, and other fintech enterprise solutions.

data.title: How NFTs Are Redefining Digital Ownership for the Next Generation
data.description:
Imagine having full digital ownership of a piece of art, a song, or even a moment in a video game—something uniquely yours that no one else can claim. That’s the promise of NFTs, or non-fungible tokens, which have surged in popularity in recent years. Unlike traditional digital files that can be copied endlessly, NFTs are unique digital assets stored on the blockchain, a type of secure online...
source: https://news.shib.io/2025/03/14/how-nfts-are-redefining-digital-ownership-for-the-next-generation/?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=how-nfts-are-redefining-digital-ownership-for-the-next-generation

data.title: Rex Shares Launches BMAX, theFirst-Ever Bitcoin Treasury Bond ETF
data.description:
TL;DR The company has launched BMAX, the first ETF offering access to convertible bonds issued by companies integrating Bitcoin into their treasury strategies. MicroStrategy leads the way with its strategy of issuing convertible bonds to acquire Bitcoin, which has inspired this ETF with 81.21% of its weighting in bonds from Michael Saylor’s firm. The launch ... 
source: https://crypto-economy.com/rex-shares-launches-bmax-thefirst-ever-bitcoin-treasury-bond-etf/

data.title: 1inch: Fraud attack impersonating official email was discovered, reminding the community to be vigilant
data.description: 1inch officially announced that it has discovered some fraudulent email attacks impersonating the official 1inch. These phishing emails appear legitimate but contain malicious links or attachments that may endanger your security, and the community is reminded to be vigilant.
